blocks.content: Component references define dependencies. Alloy uses those dependencies to decide which components to evaluate first, so the dependency graph can't contain a cycle.

When you read component references, follow each dependency in its direction. If following the references brings you back to a component you already visited, the configuration contains a cycle and is invalid.
You can now explain the no-cycles rule: a component can't reference itself directly or indirectly because Alloy needs an acyclic dependency order.

---
